thesis: Circle is the leading regulated stablecoin issuer (USDC, ~$75B circulation) with 64% revenue growth in FY2025. Revenue is interest-rate sensitive (~95% from reserve income), but the GENIUS Act creates a powerful regulatory moat. The Coinbase revenue-share agreement and extreme valuation premium (9.5x P/S) create binary risk/reward. SPEC. BUY for investors comfortable with crypto-sector volatility and Fed-policy sensitivity.

drivers: Fed funds rate stabilizes at 3.5–4.5% keeping reserve income elevated
GENIUS Act implementation creates regulated duopoly with Tether
Arc mainnet launch generates new gas-fee revenue
CPN reaches $50B+ TPV diversifying beyond reserve income

risks: Interest rates fall below 2% crushing reserve income
Coinbase renegotiates revenue share or launches competitor
USDC loses share to Tether, Ethena, or alternatives
Regulatory reversal or restrictive GENIUS Act interpretation

base_case.assumptions: USDC supply grows to $100B+ by 2027; interest rates stabilize 2.5–3.5%; GENIUS Act implemented without major restrictions; Coinbase share stays manageable; other revenue grows to 8–12%; stock trades at 8–10x forward revenue as growth normalizes.

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 Earnings — first normalized post-IPO quarter

key_risks: Interest rate sensitivity — 95%+ of revenue is reserve income tied to Fed policy
Single-product concentration — USDC dominates revenue; a depeg or regulatory action would be catastrophic
Coinbase revenue-sharing agreement eroding margins (50% of non-Coinbase interest)
Competition from Tether (USDT), bank-issued stablecoins, and Ethena
Regulatory uncertainty despite GENIUS Act progress
Extreme valuation premium (9.5x P/S, 100x+ forward P/E) prices in significant growth
